content:
💡 gaia-maker | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: A simulation game where players terraform and manage planets.
✨ Key Features: • Planet simulation based on real physics and geology • Detailed simulation of temperature, biomass, carbon cycle, insolation, and atmospheric composition • Dynamic gameplay with player actions impacting the entire planet • Resource acquisition via fusion reactors and Dyson swarms • Terraforming facilities and methods (oxygen generators, aerosol injection)
📖 Summary: Gaia Maker is a planet and terraforming simulation game developed in Rust. It offers a detailed simulation of planetary physics, geology, and environmental cycles, allowing players to influence and shape entire worlds through dynamic gameplay and various terraforming tools.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 sshm | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: Managing and connecting to SSH hosts through an interactive terminal user interface.
✨ Key Features: • Beautiful TUI Interface • Quick Connect • Port Forwarding • Easy Management • Tag Support
📖 Summary: SSHM is a Go-based command-line tool that provides a beautiful and intuitive Text User Interface (TUI) for managing SSH connections. It simplifies adding, editing, and connecting to hosts, offering features like port forwarding, tagging, and real-time status indicators.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 tlock | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: Encrypting data that should only become accessible at a specific future point in time, determined by a drand network round.
✨ Key Features: • Timelock encryption and decryption • Integration with drand threshold network • Go library and `tle` CLI tool • Support for mainnet and testnet drand networks • Production-ready mainnet with high-availability guarantees
📖 Summary: The tlock library and tle command-line tool enable practical timelock encryption and decryption using drand's threshold network. It allows users to encrypt data that can only be decrypted after a specified future round, providing a way to schedule future access to sensitive information.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
